vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as established specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but severely diminished.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Degraded: Pothos is reduced to a single, struggling sprout/stem.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: Single green shoot visible; appears to be the sole survivor of the Pothos specimen.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or lacking drainage, given the lack of growth.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of white granular material (likely perlite or mineral deposits) and a white rabbit figurine in P3.
biome_observations.desk_environment: Stable, low-light conditions; no external biological contamination observed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: Initial state shows empty pots with some debris.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Slight shift in the orientation of the P3 sprout.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Stasis; the single P3 sprout remains in the same position.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total collapse. The lack of foliage across all pots suggests a failure in the cultivation environment or extreme senescence. P3 is the only pot showing any biological activity, though it is highly precarious.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit figurine in P3 is the only non-plant intervention. The lack of expected foliage in P1, P2, and P4 is a significant negative anomaly.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently experiencing a critical failure. The registry's expected plants are absent, with the exception of a single, isolated Pothos sprout in P3. The soil appears dark and potentially waterlogged, which may have contributed to the systemic loss of the other specimens. The environment is static, with no new growth or recovery observed over the five-day period.
